summary: compliance auto-block keyword detection now matches STOP-style keywords only as standalone words, not as prefixes of unrelated tokens.
reason: The previous matcher OR-ed strings.EqualFold(body, kw), strings.Contains(body, " KW "), and strings.HasPrefix(body, KW). The HasPrefix arm matched any message that started with a keyword character sequence regardless of the next character, so a body like "STOPPER SERVICE" matched the "stop" keyword. Under --apply, the matched recipient was submitted to Bird's bulk-block endpoint -- a false-positive compliance mutation against innocent senders. Fix: replace the three string heuristics with a pre-compiled case-insensitive regex `(?i)\b(stop|stopall|unsubscribe|cancel|end|quit|optout|opt-out)\b` that anchors each keyword between word boundaries. Surfaced by Greptile P1 in the PR #417 fourth review pass.
validated_outcome: go build ./... succeeds; go test ./... passes; go vet clean.
